query: SAG-AFTRA studios streamers 2025 contract pension plan merger ratification vote January 1 2028 four-year agreement

{"query":"SAG-AFTRA studios streamers 2025 contract pension plan merger ratification vote January 1 2028 four-year agreement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1444,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://variety.com/2026/film/news/sag-aftra-ratification-ai-pension-merger-1236767288/","title":"SAG-AFTRA Members Ratify Contract With AI Terms and Pension Merger","content":"The union’s national board previously voted 89% in favor of the deal, with a handful objecting to the merger of the SAG-Producers Pension Plan and the AFTRA Retirement Fund."},{"url":"https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/business/business-news/sag-aftra-ratifies-4-year-deal-studios-1236613908/","title":"SAG-AFTRA Ratifies 4-Year Deal With Studios","content":"Members of SAG-AFTRA have ratified the four-year deal with studios and streamers that union negotiators reached in early May.
91.42 percent of voters of voted to approve the contract, while 8.58 percent were opposed.
This time around, SAG-AFTRA headed into discussions with the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers (AMPTP) focused on bolstering its AI language and wages for members.
The AMPTP got their four-year deal, while SAG-AFTRA could boast about a long-awaited plan to merge SAG-AFTRA’s two pension plans, which had remained separate since the Screen Actors Guild merged with the American Federation of Television and Radio Artists in 2012.
On compensation issues, SAG-AFTRA and the AMPTP established minimum wage rate increases of three percent for each year of the deal and a health plan contribution rate increase of 1 percent starting July 1."},{"url":"https://ground.news/article/sag-aftra-members-approve-four-year-deal-with-ai-terms-and-pension-merger","title":"Actors' Union Approves 4-Year Contract with Studios and Streamers","content":"Members approved the pact by 91.4% as it sets new AI rules and adds a 1% studio contribution to combined pension plans.
On Thursday, members of the Screen Actors Guild-American Federation of Television and Radio Artists ratified a four-year agreement with the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers, with 91.42% of voters approving the deal.
The agreement merges the SAG-Producers Pension Plan and AFTRA Retirement Fund by January 1, 2028, with studios increasing contributions by 1 percent to ensure long-term stability for members.
With the actors' deal finalized, attention shifts to the Directors Guild of America, which continues negotiations with the AMPTP ahead of its June 30 contract expiration."},{"url":"https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/tv/articles/sag-aftra-contract-includes-controversial-013347413.html","title":"SAG-AFTRA Contract Includes Controversial Merger of Pension Funds","content":"The SAG-AFTRA board on Monday approved its four-year contract with the major studios, which includes a plan to merge the union's two pension funds on Jan.
The board voted 89% in favor of recommending the deal, which now goes to the membership for a ratification vote.
The new agreement increases the figure to 35%.
The deal also includes increases to most minimum rates by 3% for each of the four years of the contract.
SAG-AFTRA negotiators reached a deal on May 3 after six weeks of bargaining with the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers.
The Directors Guild of America sat down Monday with the AMPTP to begin its round of negotiations, which are expected to last through early June."}]}
